title: mustardy icy korean rice songpyeon
description: a comforting korean jeolla dish featuring freshly chopped parsley, made with ayurvedic sattvic ingredients and a hint of mustard.
instructions: in a medium saucepan, combine the jasmine rice and 1 cup of water. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes.
in a small pan, toast the mustard seeds over low heat, stirring frequently, until fragrant and lightly browned.
in a blender or food processor, combine the toasted mustard seeds, sesame oil, soy milk, tofu, and chopped parsley. Blend until smooth.
add the blended mixture to the cooked jasmine rice and stir-fry over medium heat for 2-3 minutes.
add the kombu to the rice mixture and stir-fry for another minute.
transfer the rice mixture to a baking dish and broil for 5-7 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned and crispy.
remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before serving.
